signature: def generate_cliff_face_mesh(width=20.0, height=15.0, segments_horizontal=16, segments_vertical=12, noise_amplitude=0.8, noise_scale=3.0, seed=0, style='granite') -> MeshSpec:
aaa_equivalent: Megascans cliff scans / Quixel rock kit / Gaea Rock node
strength: Deterministic grid with per-vertex Gaussian displacement; returns clean MeshSpec.
weakness: Single Gaussian-noise pass with sinusoidal mask — no fracture planes, no strata layering, no overhangs. AAA cliffs use scanned heightfields or rule-based joint systems.
upgrade: Add stacked strata noise, fault-plane displacement, overhang support (Y can exceed 0.3*sin curve), and weathered talus at base.

signature: def generate_cave_entrance_mesh(width=4.0, height=4.0, depth=3.0, arch_segments=12, terrain_edge_height=0.0, style='natural', seed=0) -> MeshSpec:
aaa_equivalent: UE5 Nanite cave kit / Skyrim cave entrance module
strength: Builds a real tunnel ring-loft with natural jitter; correct topology for quad faces.
weakness: Symmetric semicircular arch, uniform extrusion — no irregular mouth shape, no stalactites/lintel erosion, no interior branching.
upgrade: Randomize arch shape (non-semicircle), vary ring profile along depth, add debris apron + rock framing.

signature: def generate_biome_transition_mesh(biome_a='forest', biome_b='swamp', zone_width=10.0, zone_depth=20.0, segments=12, seed=0) -> MeshSpec:
aaa_equivalent: UE Landscape layer blend / Witcher 3 biome transition
strength: Writes vertex_groups for blend weight — shader-pipeline aware.
weakness: Flat plane with trivial Gaussian bumps. Blend is linear across x. No actual biome-specific geometry (roots, moss hummocks, puddles).
upgrade: Height should sample from biome-specific heightmap; blend factor should be noise-displaced not straight-linear.

signature: def generate_waterfall_mesh(width=3.0, height=10.0, steps=4, step_depth=0.5, pool_radius=2.0, style='rocky_cascade', seed=0) -> MeshSpec:
aaa_equivalent: Horizon Forbidden West waterfall / RDR2 volumetric cascade
strength: Produces stepped cascade + pool disk — 3D volume (per user's 'never flat plane' rule for falls).
weakness: Curtain is a flat-ish two-row strip (checks feedback_waterfall_must_have_volume — this IS almost flat). Pool is a single-segment fan disk. No mist zone, no foam edge, no thickness taper — exactly the failure mode called out in memory.
upgrade: Curtain needs front curvature >=3 segments, thickness taper top->bottom, plunge pool needs bowl not flat disc. Integrate with terrain_waterfalls_volumetric profile.

signature: def detect_cliff_edges(heightmap, slope_threshold_deg=60.0, min_cluster_size=4, terrain_size=100.0, height_scale=1.0) -> list[dict]:
aaa_equivalent: Houdini Edge Detection / Gaea Slope mask with clustering
strength: Real slope-map gradient + flood-fill clustering + gradient-derived facing angle. Includes dimensional-units bug fix (TERR-001) with proper height_scale propagation.
weakness: Python for-loop flood-fill is O(N) but slow on large grids; Dr. cluster rotation uses only center cell gradient (not PCA over cluster); no confidence/overlap score.
upgrade: Switch to scipy.ndimage.label for O(HW) label pass and fit oriented bounding box via PCA of cluster cells for rotation.

signature: def derive_pass_seed(intent_seed, seed_namespace, tile_x, tile_y, region) -> int:
aaa_equivalent: Houdini HDA deterministic seed chaining / UE5 PCG seed domain
strength: SHA-256 of JSON-encoded tuple, PYTHONHASHSEED-independent, 32-bit masked for numpy RNG. Production-correct.
weakness: JSON encoding adds overhead vs a direct bytes hash, but negligible.
upgrade: Cache on intent to avoid re-hashing per-pass in tight loops if needed.

signature: def run_pass(self, pass_name, region=None, *, force=False, checkpoint=True) -> PassResult:
aaa_equivalent: Houdini TOP pass executor / UE5 PCG graph node runner
strength: Enforces scene-read, protected zones, input-channel prereqs, post-run produces_channels verification, quality-gate with hard/soft severity, visual_validator, content-hash before/after, checkpoint on success. Very thorough contract enforcement.
weakness: Failure in the pass records duration but the state may be partly mutated — no transactional rollback of mask_stack. Pragma: no cover on the except clause hides untested error paths.
upgrade: Snapshot mask_stack before func() and restore on exception (use checkpoint dir already present).

aaa_equivalent: UE Landscape layer exclusion / Horizon zero region lock
strength: Correctly distinguishes full-cover vs partial intersection; lets per-cell masks do finer policy.
weakness: Region BBox is axis-aligned only — polygon zones not supported.
upgrade: Accept polygon zones for hero-art protection (tournament-quality Skyrim-like zone masks).

signature: def run_pipeline(self, intent=None, pass_sequence=None, *, region=None, checkpoint=True) -> List[PassResult]:
strength: Default sequence is sensible (macro->masks->erosion->validate). Stops on first failure.
weakness: No parallel tile dispatch; no partial rollback to last-good checkpoint on failure.
upgrade: Add multiprocessing per-tile and auto-rollback option.

aaa_equivalent: Houdini node cache / UE5 asset version checkpointing
strength: Captures Unity-export metadata (world_bounds, height range, cell_size, coord system) so checkpoints are round-trippable. Parent linkage for undo chain.
weakness: NPZ files pile up over long sessions — no rotation/pruning.
upgrade: Add checkpoint retention policy.

strength: Declarative definitions with requires/produces channels — enables static contract verification.
weakness: Hard-coded 4 passes; no way to compose profiles.
upgrade: Load profile from YAML to support game-specific pass graphs.

strength: Verifies height channel and records metrics. Idempotent.
weakness: Docstring admits it's effectively a no-op — height is populated at state construction. A real macro pass should orchestrate tectonic base + regional distortion. Currently it's a validator masquerading as a generator.
upgrade: Actually call generate_world_heightmap from authoring intent here, so macro_world owns the base terrain, not state construction.

strength: Delegates to terrain_masks.compute_base_masks which produces slope/curvature/concavity/convexity/ridge/basin/saliency — all the analysis channels a AAA pipeline expects.
weakness: supports_region_scope=False (always full-tile) which is correct but expensive; saliency_macro is a single scalar metric name rather than documented formula.
upgrade: Doc each channel's formula and add tests that compare against a Houdini reference.

strength: Layered approach: analytical erosion (chunk-parallel deterministic) -> hydraulic droplet -> thermal talus. Respects protected zones per-cell, region scoping with pre-snapshot restore. Per-profile (temperate/arid/alpine) config. Derives deterministic seed.
weakness: Default iterations 200-600 are low vs Gaea's 10k+; no explicit sediment transport conservation check; thermal uses only 6 iterations always.
upgrade: Expose iteration scaling with tile resolution; add mass-balance validation.

strength: Finite-check on height and NaN/inf scan across channels — solid minimal invariants.
weakness: Truly minimal — no range checks (height bounds), no monotone drainage validation, no seam validation with neighbors.
upgrade: Add range + seam checks so 'validation' earns the name.

signature: def generate_world_heightmap(width, height, *, scale=100.0, world_origin_x=0.0, world_origin_y=0.0, cell_size=1.0, seed=0, terrain_type='mountains', normalize=False, ...) -> np.ndarray:
aaa_equivalent: Gaea canvas sampler at world window
strength: Deterministic world-window sampling with world origin + cell size — tile-safe by default. normalize=False preserves the contract.
weakness: Thin wrapper around generate_heightmap; assumes single-scale noise; no macro/meso/micro composition.
upgrade: Compose tectonic plates + regional distortion + local detail as separate noise layers (Gaea Mountain+Hills+Rocks).

signature: def erode_world_heightmap(heightmap, *, hydraulic_iterations=1000, thermal_iterations=0, seed=0, talus_angle=40.0, cell_size=1.0) -> dict:
strength: Single-region erosion preserving source domain (no normalize/denormalize round-trip), returns flow metadata.
weakness: Default 1000 hydraulic iterations is far below AAA (Gaea 10k+ for visible gully detail). Thermal only if explicitly asked.
upgrade: Raise default to 10k+; auto-scale with resolution.

aaa_equivalent: Horizon Forbidden West river-lip detector / Houdini flow-based feature tagging
strength: Real drainage+drop joint threshold, D8 descent, per-cell confidence blending drainage and drop scores, D8-neighbor dedup to collapse lip rows.
weakness: Python nested for-loop — slow on >1k grids; confidence is linear blend (could use logistic).
upgrade: Vectorize with numpy argmax over D8 stack and np.where thresholds.

aaa_equivalent: Houdini cascade solver / RDR2 waterfall chain authoring
strength: Traces plunge path with plateau detection, records multi-tier drop segments, derives pool radius from sqrt(total_drop), computes outflow direction, generates deterministic chain_id from world position.
weakness: Plateau detection is heuristic (plateau_hits>=2); outflow cap of 32 cells may truncate long cascades; no actual overhang geometry — chain is centerline only.
upgrade: Add overhang detection at lip (undercut carving), longer outflow cap, multi-drop merging with confluence.

strength: Parabolic bowl is correct — center deepest, 0 at rim. Non-destructive delta approach respects pipeline protocol.
weakness: Circular footprint; real plunge pools have directional scour (downstream elongation) from jet impact.
upgrade: Elliptical footprint oriented along outflow_direction_rad; erode lip downstream.

strength: Pushes shallow trench along outflow waypoints with proper min-delta merging (keeps deepest).
weakness: Square-kernel iteration per waypoint (Python double loop) — slow; linear taper rather than parabolic; no meander.
upgrade: Vectorize with scipy.ndimage.distance_transform_edt across path mask; add meander.

aaa_equivalent: Horizon Forbidden West localized volumetric fog
strength: Radial falloff from pool center, returns float32 for shader consumption.
weakness: Perfect circle — wind direction not modeled; Python inner loops.
upgrade: Bias mist downwind from chain's outflow vector; vectorize.

strength: Scales foam by chain.foam_intensity (which is tied to total_drop) — physically motivated.
weakness: Same radial profile as mist; no foam along plunge path itself, only pool.
upgrade: Add foam along full plunge_path and outflow with turbulence-derived intensity.

strength: Enforces min vertex density per meter, non-zero thickness, front_curvature >=3 — directly addresses user's 'must be 3D volumetric' rule from memory.
weakness: Only checks profile params, not actual mesh generated — relies on downstream to honor profile.
upgrade: Round-trip check: generate mesh, measure vert density, feed back into validation.

aaa_equivalent: Gaea hydrology graph node / RDR2 river pass
strength: Full chain: lips -> solve -> carve pools -> outflow -> foam/mist masks -> wet_rock integration with water_network. Applies pool_delta to height (real mutation). Region scoping. Fixes pipeline-breaks #1, #2, #5 per comments. Issues validated and downgraded to warning.
weakness: Caps at 16 chains (soft limit); wet_rock merging uses max instead of accumulate.
upgrade: Parameterize cap; consider alpha-blend wet_rock accumulation.

signature: def generate_cave_path(stack, archetype, entrance_pos, seed) -> List[Tuple[float, float, float]]:
aaa_equivalent: Houdini curve-based cave authoring / UE5 Voxel Farm cave curve
strength: Per-archetype path shape (LAVA_TUBE straight, FISSURE vertical, KARST plunge-then-arm, GLACIAL meander, SEA_GROTTO shallow). Deterministic rng.
weakness: All paths are 2D polylines with simple trigonometric jitter — no branching, no chamber nodes, no inclination variation within archetype.
upgrade: Add branching (Y-splits), chamber hubs along path, per-archetype branch probability.

aaa_equivalent: Voxel carve along spline (UE5 Voxel Plugin)
strength: Proper vectorized np.mgrid footprint per waypoint; taper by distance from path center; deepest-delta-per-cell merge; populates cave_candidate bool mask.
weakness: Flat 2D footprint — heightmap can't represent true 3D caves (the delta only lowers terrain surface). For real caves this needs voxel/mesh-boolean backend.
upgrade: Wire through to a voxel field (terrain_advanced) so caves are true 3D volumes.

strength: Per-archetype framing count (2 or 3), computes lintel position from entrance_height, vegetation_screen predicate, occlusion_shelf intent.
weakness: Framing rocks are just world positions + radius — no actual shape variation, no per-archetype material hint.
upgrade: Specify rock asset kit per archetype + size variance + biased noise for position.

signature: def scatter_collapse_debris(stack, path, spec, seed) -> List[Tuple[float, float, float]]:
strength: Deterministic, density-scaled, normal-jitter around path; caps count 200 to bound work.
weakness: No clustering (debris typically piles), no collision with entrance frame; all debris same type.
upgrade: Add Poisson clustering + per-cluster type.

strength: Vectorized np.mgrid per waypoint with proper max-merge; preserves existing wet_rock.
weakness: Linear falloff — real damp patterns follow seepage paths (downslope preference).
upgrade: Bias falloff downslope of path.

strength: End-to-end: scene_read-derived entrances, per-cave deterministic seed via hash-mix, protected-zone cell check, archetype pick, path+carve+frame+debris+damp, cave_candidate protected masking, side_effects recorded for downstream bundles, validation.
weakness: Only processes scene-read supplied entrances (no auto-discovery from heightmap features); cell_count recomputed per loop.
upgrade: Add auto-entrance detection using cliff-edge + slope + basin proximity when scene_read is empty.

aaa_equivalent: MCP handler shim over cave authoring
strength: Bridges params dict to pass_caves via synthetic state; returns debris/frame geometry intent.
weakness: Uses synthetic state builder — real scene_read may not be present; chamber mesh is a simple box loft.
upgrade: Prefer actual scene state over synthetic; richer chamber mesh.

signature: def generate_coastline(length=200.0, width=50.0, style='rocky', resolution=64, seed=42) -> dict:
aaa_equivalent: SpeedTree coastal kit / World Creator coast node
strength: Four styles (rocky/sandy/cliffs/harbor) with distinct shore profiles + feature kits + material zones; validates inputs; returns mesh + features + material_zones + shoreline_profile.
weakness: Uses hash-based pseudo-noise (_hash_noise) which is low-quality compared to OpenSimplex; style-specific elevation logic is hardcoded if/elif chain; cliff z-step is a discrete jump ('if land_factor > 0.3: z = base_elev * min(1,...)').
upgrade: Swap hash_noise for proper 2D OpenSimplex; smooth cliff z-profile; parameterize per-style in a config dict.

aaa_equivalent: Sea of Thieves wave-energy modeling / Houdini coastal exposure analysis
strength: Real physical model: shoreline band (gaussian around sea level), directional exposure from gradient dot-product with wave direction, clipped properly. Vectorized numpy.
weakness: Hardcoded 5m decay constant for shoreline band; no fetch-length modeling.
upgrade: Parameterize decay; add fetch (distance from incoming wave source).

strength: Recomputes wave energy on demand; respects rock_hardness for differential erosion (softer erodes more).
weakness: hints_wave_dir=0.0 hardcoded — should pull from state hints; max 3m drop per pass is fixed.
upgrade: Thread wave dir through and parameterize max drop.

strength: Clean vectorized band + linear taper outside; writes to stack.tidal; accepts zero-range gracefully.
weakness: Linear taper; real intertidal zones have exponential drainage patterns.
upgrade: Exponential taper optional; integrate with wet_rock merge.

strength: Wires hints (sea_level, tidal_range, wave_dir, apply_retreat) from state.intent.composition_hints — composable. Reports wave energy metrics.
weakness: Doesn't actually mutate height even when coastal_erosion_enabled — it stores delta but carving is the caller's job. Metrics advertise tidal_coverage_fraction, good.
upgrade: Option to apply the delta directly like pass_waterfalls does with pool_delta.

aaa_equivalent: UE5 volumetric fog / Horizon Forbidden West localized fog sheets
strength: Per-biome rules, area-based count calculation using volume footprint per shape, deterministic.
weakness: Uniform random placement — no clustering around terrain features (god_rays should emit from tree gaps, fireflies near water). No occlusion/collision awareness. Volume area math for cone uses (h*0.5)^2 — arbitrary approximation.
upgrade: Accept heightmap + masks (ridge, water, canopy) and bias placement by feature affinity per volume type.

strength: Three shape branches (box, 12-vertex icosphere-like, 8-segment cone). Deterministic.
weakness: Icosphere is actually a dodecahedron arrangement (uses 12 verts but faces have errors — 'next_next if next_next <= segments else 1' in cone logic is suspicious and produces degenerate tris). Mesh resolution is too coarse for any close-up.
upgrade: Replace with proper icosphere subdivision (subdiv=1 gives 42 verts); fix cone face generation math.

strength: Simple linear cost model + threshold-based recommendation. Easy to tune.
weakness: Uses arbitrary thresholds (15/30/60/100) with no ms-budget grounding; assumes all particle/distortion volumes cost the same.
upgrade: Calibrate against actual GPU profile data from a reference scene.

signature: def generate_world_map_spec(width=256, height=256, world_size=512.0, biome_count=6, biomes=None, seed=42, corruption_level=0.0, building_plots=None, transition_width_m=15.0) -> WorldMapSpec:
aaa_equivalent: UE5 PCG World Partition biome graph / Witcher 3 region splitter
strength: Voronoi biome distribution with soft transitions, corruption fBm map with separate seed, building-plot flatten zones with padded radius, per-biome climate params, name resolution via alias dict.
weakness: Voronoi is fixed-weight — no biome-count rebalancing based on temperature/moisture gradients; flatten zones are circular only.
upgrade: Climate-driven Voronoi weighting; rectangular flatten zones for linear building footprints.

signature: def apply_periglacial_patterns(heightmap, seed=0, intensity=0.5, frost_heave_scale=0.02) -> np.ndarray:
strength: Voronoi cell boundaries as polygon ridges — correct physics intuition for permafrost pattern.
weakness: Python loop over n_centers for distance (O(N*H*W)) — scales poorly; uses np.random.RandomState (legacy); no orientation (real stone stripes align with slope).
upgrade: Use KDTree or broadcast to single numpy op; add slope-aligned striping.

signature: def apply_desert_pavement(heightmap, seed=0, intensity=0.5, smoothing_radius=3) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
strength: Slope+elevation mask combo for pavement zones; returns mask for shader use; box filter in numpy (no scipy).
weakness: Pure-numpy box filter via _box_filter_2d has O(H*W) Python loop — slow on 1k+ grids.
upgrade: Use np.cumsum-based vectorized integral-image computation.

strength: Mid-slope band + elevation-contour boundaries — correct two-factor filter for spring emergence.
weakness: Layer boundaries placed by elevation quantization — real geology uses stratigraphic dip, not level contours. Narrow bands (exp/0.001) can miss entirely at some resolutions.
upgrade: Use actual stratigraphy (from terrain_stratigraphy) rather than quantized elevation.

signature: def apply_landslide_scars(heightmap, seed=0, num_slides=3, scar_depth=0.05, runout_factor=2.5) -> np.ndarray:
strength: Slope-weighted origin sampling, concave scar + convex deposit fan with 60% volume conservation heuristic — geologically motivated.
weakness: Scar mask is concentric circle (uphill vs downhill not asymmetric); deposit placement uses gradient at origin only — doesn't flow along actual descent path.
upgrade: Trace downslope path and distribute deposits along travel line; elongate scar uphill only.

signature: def apply_hot_spring_features(heightmap, seed=0, num_springs=2, pool_radius=5.0, pool_depth=0.03, terrace_rings=4) -> tuple[np.ndarray, list[dict]]:
aaa_equivalent: Yellowstone travertine terraces reference (Hell Let Loose / Horizon)
strength: Pool depression + concentric terrace rings with diminishing step_h — readable geology pattern. Returns spring metadata for VFX wiring.
weakness: Perfect circular rings; real travertine is asymmetric (downhill-biased, irregular ridges).
upgrade: Skew rings downhill; perturb ring_r by noise.

signature: def apply_reef_platform(heightmap, sea_level=0.0, seed=0, reef_width=8.0, reef_height=0.01) -> np.ndarray:
aaa_equivalent: Horizon Forbidden West coral reef authoring
strength: Uses _distance_from_mask transform; reef band centered on half-width; clamps to sea_level. Honest early-return when coastline absent.
weakness: Uniform reef around whole coast; real reefs are biased toward warm/shallow-gradient shores.
upgrade: Gate by tidal_range and distance-from-land to avoid reefs in deep-water-hugging coasts.

signature: def apply_tafoni_weathering(heightmap, seed=0, intensity=0.5, cavity_scale=0.01, num_cavities=50) -> np.ndarray:
strength: Steep-slope weighted sampling, elliptical cavity shape; proper intensity gate.
weakness: 50 cavities over full heightmap is sparse — honeycomb weathering is by definition dense; cavity depth flat (no interior texture).
upgrade: Scale cavity count with steep area; layer micro+macro cavities.

signature: def apply_geological_folds(heightmap, seed=0, num_folds=3, amplitude=0.05, wavelength_cells=30.0, fold_type='syncline') -> np.ndarray:
strength: Three fold types (anticline/syncline/chevron), random strike direction, chevron uses triangular wave.
weakness: Adds folds linearly — real tectonic folds scale fold amplitude with cumulative strain and plunge along axis.
upgrade: Add plunging-fold support (amplitude varies along strike).

signature: def compute_river_width(flow_accumulation, min_width=1.0, max_width=20.0, scale_factor=0.002) -> float:
strength: Correct sqrt scaling — width ~ sqrt(discharge) is the accepted hydraulic-geometry relation. Min/max clamp.
weakness: Scale factor is hardcoded default; no regional-calibration hook.
upgrade: Expose scale_factor via biome/climate params.

signature: def trace_river_from_flow(flow_direction, flow_accumulation, start_row, start_col, min_accumulation=500.0) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
strength: D8 descent with visited-set, pit detection, accumulation-drop guard against infinite loops.
weakness: Uses Python list for path — okay for single rivers; min_accumulation check '> 0' allows starting cell below threshold.
upgrade: Vectorize for bulk source processing.

strength: Pit detection + flood-fill to spill height + area/inflow records; correct set-based visited tracking.
weakness: Naive pit detection (cell < all 8 neighbors) misses plateau lakes where multiple cells share min; spill_z is lowest neighbor, not true watershed spill.
upgrade: Use priority-flood algorithm (Barnes 2014) for real watershed spill computation.

signature: def detect_waterfalls(heightmap, river_path, cell_size=1.0, min_drop=3.0, max_horizontal=5.0) -> list[dict]:
strength: Sliding-window scan along a river path, finds best drop in bounded horizontal distance, skips past detected falls.
weakness: Only scans explicit river_path — won't find falls off the given path; best_drop is scalar, not rate (drop/horiz).
upgrade: Score by drop rate (drop/horiz_dist); also run independently on drainage raster.

strength: Four-zone classification (ground/slopes/cliffs/water_edges) per-face via normal + height + water_level; distributes across palette materials by face index for variance.
weakness: Modulo face index distribution is deterministic but visually linear — no spatial coherence (adjacent faces can flip material).
upgrade: Use spatial hash (face center) with blue noise for variance.

signature: def blend_terrain_vertex_colors(mesh_data, biome_name) -> list[tuple[float, float, float, float]]:
strength: Averages per-vertex zone weights across adjacent faces; normalizes RGBA to sum 1; isolated-vertex fallback.
weakness: Static zone->channel weights ignore biome-specific preferences (a swamp shouldn't give .6 grass + 0 rock uniformly); no smoothing across vertices.
upgrade: Pull zone weights per-biome from palette; Laplacian smooth.

signature: def compute_biome_transition(vertices, face_normals, faces, biome_a, biome_b, transition_width=20.0, boundary_axis='x', boundary_position=0.0, noise_scale=0.1, noise_amplitude=5.0, noise_seed=42) -> list[tuple[float, float, float, float]]:
strength: Runs auto_assign_terrain_layers for both biomes then smoothsteps between them with noise-displaced boundary — clean organic edge.
weakness: Axis-aligned boundary only (x or y); _simple_noise_2d is a hash-based approximation, not true Simplex; amplitude is in world units but scale is normalized.
upgrade: Support arbitrary polyline boundary + OpenSimplex noise.

signature: def compute_world_splatmap_weights(heightmap, biome_name=DEFAULT_BIOME, *, cell_size=1.0, slope_flat_deg=30.0, slope_cliff_deg=60.0, special_low_pct=0.15, special_high_pct=0.85, moisture_map=None, height_range=None) -> np.ndarray:
strength: Fully vectorized numpy (previously Python double loop — now <200ms on 512² per docstring), height+slope+moisture fusion, special-band policy with ground-restriction, moisture tint, final normalization preserving alpha. This is production quality.
weakness: Linear slope thresholds; no anisotropic lighting hint; moisture tint is limited (>0.7 or <0.3).
upgrade: Soften slope transition with smoothstep; expose moisture curve.

signature: def apply_corruption_tint(vertex_colors, corruption_level) -> list[tuple[float, float, float, float]]:
strength: Straightforward RGBA blend toward corruption purple; pushes alpha to special channel.
weakness: Uniform tint — no per-vertex corruption variance; hardcoded purple (0.12, 0.04, 0.14) from 'linear sRGB' comment.
upgrade: Sample corruption_map per-vertex for spatial variance.

aaa_equivalent: UE5 Landscape editor full procedural pipeline
strength: Dual-path (controller vs legacy) with fallback; VB biome preset resolution with override logic; AAA erosion minimum (150K droplets) matches user's quality bar; controller pass sequence composition with missing-pass graceful skip; flatten_zones, cliff_overlays, moisture_map, object_location respect; returns full metadata including heightmap and cell_size for downstream.
weakness: Massive handler (330+ lines) — hard to test; legacy path bypasses controller entirely (risk of divergent behavior); moisture_map logic only runs on legacy path.
upgrade: Extract into smaller orchestration functions; unify legacy and controller paths.

strength: Proper tile coords + cell_size + world origin threading for seam-safety.
weakness: Not inspected in full, but naming + params suggest proper world-tiled authoring.
upgrade: Assert seam validation against neighbor tiles after generation.

strength: Owns the 'one world heightmap' path — complements tile-based flow.
weakness: Not deeply inspected — but the dual (world vs tile) interface is a code-duplication risk.
upgrade: Share core with tile version via a core generator.

strength: Blender-side wrapper for the pure controller.run_pass; lets agents iterate on individual passes.
weakness: Relies on controller state living across calls — session-scoped state management not explicitly checked here.
upgrade: Add explicit session id param for multi-map workflows.

strength: Proper error-first guard: requires heightmap/water-network context unless explicit legacy opt-in; picks closest candidate by world-pos; derives facing_direction from top->bottom vector; wires functional_objects (river/pool/mist/foam/wet_rock/particles) with naming contract enforcement; publishes real Blender objects with material.
weakness: Still calls legacy generate_waterfall as fallback — deprecated geometry flagged but reachable. Functional_objects_materialized path has silent except.
upgrade: Remove legacy fallback entirely after grace period; stop swallowing materialize errors.

strength: Handler exists for a critical multi-tile operation.
weakness: Docstring says 'Fallback seam stitcher' — suggests this is the backup path, real seams handled elsewhere; not inspected in full.
upgrade: Fold into the primary tile pipeline if possible.

strength: Named for its job; ties into material system.
weakness: Not inspected in full.
upgrade: Verify it respects biome palette and vertex-color splatmap contract.

aaa_equivalent: Houdini river carve along spline / Gaea River node
strength: Extracts heightmap from mesh, uses _detect_grid_dims (non-square safe), WorldHeightTransform for signed-range preservation (no corruption of negative-elevation worlds), waypointed multi-segment carve, derives river surface Z separately from bed Z, smooths both, returns path_points and bed_points in world coords.
weakness: Uses bmesh per-vertex update which is slow on dense terrain; depth param interpretation has a normalization clamp.
upgrade: Batch vertex updates via foreach_set.

strength: Handler exists and is wired.
weakness: Not deeply inspected — grading by association with generate_cave_entrance_mesh (C).
upgrade: Upgrade geometry via terrain_caves path integration.

aaa_equivalent: UE5 Landmass road tool / Gaea Path
strength: Has crown-and-ditch profile (_apply_road_profile_to_heightmap inspected — real cross-section deformation with outer_radius for shoulder), grade-strength, proper segment iteration with distance-to-segment math.
weakness: Not deeply inspected for full signature; linear interpolation for segments (no spiral easing).
upgrade: Add clothoid easing; bank roads on grades.

aaa_equivalent: UE5 Water System (SingleLayerWater shader + spline river)
strength: Spline-following cross-section mesh; vertex color layer 'flow_vc' encoding bank contact + flow dir XY + foam in RGBA; UV tiling by arc length; terrain-aware bank height sampler; terminal-taper; preview_fast/surface_only modes; smoothed flow speeds; river_bank_contact resolver with side_sign. This is genuine AAA-track authoring.
weakness: Still falls back to simple grid when no path_points; many params (20+) suggests feature bloat; _build_terrain_world_height_sampler builds a new sampler per call.
upgrade: Cache sampler per terrain object.

strength: Named handler exists with clear intent.
weakness: Not deeply inspected.
upgrade: Verify basin carves respect protected zones and flow accumulation.

aaa_equivalent: Unity Terrain RAW 16-bit LE importer / UE Landscape import
strength: Purpose-built for Unity RAW16 LE export per CLAUDE.md protocol.
weakness: Not deeply inspected.
upgrade: Add auto-validation of exported file (dims/endianness).

strength: Name and integration with WorldMapSpec indicate real multi-biome composition.

aaa_equivalent: UE5 PCG vegetation / SpeedTree foliage pipeline
strength: Poisson disk sampling + biome-filtered placement + slope rejection + moisture filter + building/road exclusion zones with proportional margins + collection instances (not individual objects) for performance + per-template billboard LOD setup. Production-oriented.

upgrade: Spatial hash for exclusion; hysteresis on LOD switch.
